Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 013 [Šurpu 2][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

[...] x [...]

(o i 1') [...] ... [...];

o i 2'2'

[a-na ba]-⸢ni⸣-tu₄ le-⸢mut⸣-[tu₄ ... <<BU>>] 1

(o i 2') [...] misfortune [to] good fortune;

o i 3'3'

[a-na] ⸢le⸣-mut-tu₄ ba-ni-tu₄ [... <<BU>>]

(o i 3') [...] good fortune [to] misfortune;

o i 4'4'

[...]-ni ṣa-lip₂-tu₄ u₂-[šad-bi-bu <<BU>>]

(o i 4') [...] ..., who caused dishonesty to be spoken;

o i 5'5'

[...]-tu₂ da-a-a-nu u₂-[ša₂-ḫi-zu <<BU>>]

(o i 5') Who caused the judge to learn [...] ...;

o i 6'6'

x [...] x ab-ta-a-tu₂ iz-[za-az-zu <<BU>>]

(o i 6') Who stands [...] ... ruins;

o i 7'7'

i-du-u₂ la i-qab-bu-[u₂ <<BU>>]

(o i 7') Who knows (something, but) doesn't say (it);

o i 8'8'

i-qab-bu-u₂ ut-ta-⸢ru?⸣ [<<BU>>]

(o i 8') Who says (something, but) exaggerates;

o i 9'9'

en-su ḫab-lu e-niš-tu₂ i-da-ṣu [<<BU>>]

(o i 9') Who treated unjustly the wronged weak man, the weak woman;

o i 10'10'

al-mat-tu₄ ina di-i-ni IRI-šu₂ u₂-saḫ-ḫi-ru [<<BU>>]

(o i 10') Who turned away a widow in the lawsuit of his city;

o i 11'11'

it-ti AD DUMU ip-ru-us [<<BU>>]

(o i 11') Who alienated son from father;

o i 12'12'

it-ti DUMU AD ip-ru-us [<<BU>>]

(o i 12') Who alienated father from son;

o i 13'13'

it-ti AMA DUMU.MUNUS ip-ru-us [<<BU>>]

(o i 13') Who alienated daughter from mother;

o i 14'14'

it-ti DUMU.MUNUS AMA ip-ru-us (o) <<⸢BU⸣>>

(o i 14') Who alienated mother from daughter;

o i 15'15'

it-ti e-me-tu₄ kal-la-tu₄ ip-ru-us <<BU>>

(o i 15') Who alienated daughter-in-law from mother-in-law;

o i 16'16'

it-ti kal-la-tu₄ e-me-tu₄ ip-ru-us <<BU>>

(o i 16') Who alienated mother-in-law from daughter-in-law;

o i 17'17'

it-ti ŠEŠ ŠEŠ-šu₂ ip-ru-us <<BU>>

(o i 17') Who alienated his brother from a brother;

o i 18'18'

it-ti ib-ri i-bir-šu₂ ip-ru-us <<BU>>

(o i 18') Who alienated his friend from a friend;

o i 19'19'

it-ti ru-u₈-a ru-u₈-a ip-ru-su <<BU>> : ṣab-tu₄ la u₂-maš-ši-ru <<BU>>

(o i 19') Who alienated colleague from colleague : who didn't release the captive;

o i 20'20'

ka-sa-a la u₂-ram-mu-u₂ <<BU>>

(o i 20') Who didn't free the prisoner;

o i 21'21'

ša₂ E₂ ṣi-bit-tu₄ la u₂-kal-li-mu nu-u₂-ru <<BU>>

(o i 21') Who didn't show light to the prisoner;

o i 22'22'

ana ṣab-tu₄ ṣa-bat-su-ma!(U₂) <<BU>>

(o i 22') Who said to the captive: "seize him," (and) to the bound man, "bind him";

o i 23'23'

ana ka-sa-a ku!(KA-)si-<<ši>>-šu-ma iq-bu-u₂ <<BU>>

o i 24'24'

ul i-di šer₃-ti i-lu <<BU>>

(o i 24') He doesn't know a crime against the god;

o i 25'25'

ul i-di en-net d15 <<BU>>

(o i 25') He doesn't know a sin against the goddess;

o i 26'26'

DINGIR-MEŠ i-da-ṣi d15 im-ta-ši <<BU>>

(o i 26') He treated the gods unjustly, disregarded the goddess;

o i 27'27'

a-na DINGIR-MEŠ-šu₂ ar₂-ni-šu₂ ana d15-šu₂ gil₂-lat-su <<BU>>

(o i 27') His transgressions are against his gods, his sins are against his goddess;

o i 28'28'

a-na be-en-nu da-ṣa-a-tu₄ <<BU>>

(o i 28') Treachery against son;

o i 29'29'

⸢a-na⸣ ŠEŠ GALu₂ ze-ra-a-tu₄ <<BU>>

(o i 29') Hostilities against elder brother;

o i 30'30'

[AD] ⸢u?⸣ AMA im-te-eš <<BU>>

(o i 30') He disregarded [father] and mother;

o i 31'31'

[a-na NIN GAL]ti ug-dal-lil <<BU>>

(o i 31') He sins [against] elder [sister];

o i 32'32'

[ina ṣe-ḫer]-⸢ti⸣ it-ta-din ina ra-bi-tu₄ im-ta-ḫar <<BU>>

(o i 32') He gave [by] the small unit, received by the large unit;

o i 33'33'

[a-na ia-aʾ]-nu i-ba-aš₂-ši iq-ta-bi <<BU>>

(o i 33') He said "there is" [for] "there is not";

Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

⸢bar⸣-[ru ...]

(o ii 1') ... [...];

o ii 2'2'

la ⸢am⸣-[ra-a-ti ...]

(o ii 2') unseemly (things) [...];

o ii 3'3'

la na-[ṭa-ti ...]

(o ii 3') unsuitable (things) [...];

o ii 4'4'

ar₂-ki [...]

(o ii 4') after [...];

o ii 5'5'

i-te-e [...]

(o ii 5') the boundary [...];

o ii 6'6'

la ba-ni-[tu₄ ...]

(o ii 6') something bad [...];

o ii 7'7'

a-na kiš-⸢pi⸣ [...]

(o ii 7') To sorcery [...];

o ii 8'8'

ina NIG₂.⸢GIG⸣ [...]

(o ii 8') Because of the taboo [...];

o ii 9'9'

ina ar₂-nu [...]

(o ii 9') Because of the sin [...];

o ii 10'10'

ina ⸢pu⸣-[uḫ-ri ...]

(o ii 10') Because of the assembly [...];

o ii 11'11'

⸢ina⸣ [...]

(o ii 11') Because of [...];

o ii 12'12'

⸢ina⸣ [...]

(o ii 12') Because of [...];

o ii 13'13'

⸢ina⸣ [...]

(o ii 13') Because of [...];

(rest of column missing)
Reverse
Column i
(1 column missing)
Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

[...] EN EDIN <<BU>>

(r ii 1) [...], lord of open country;

r ii 22

[...] EN tap-šer₃-ti <<BU>>

(r ii 2) [...], lord of magical release;

r ii 33

[... dšu-qa-mu]-nu u dši-ma-li-ia <<BU>>

(r ii 3) [...] Šuqamunu and Šimaliya;

r ii 44

[... DINGIR]-MEŠ ša₂ ANe u KIti₃ ma-la MU-šu₂-nu za-kar <<BU>>

(r ii 4) [...], gods of heaven and earth, as many as are named (by) their name;

r ii 55

[... ki]-nu-nu DUMU de₂-a—LUGAL <<BU>>

(r ii 5) [...], Brazier, son of Ea-šarru;

r ii 66

[dEN] u dGAŠAN lip-ṭu-ru <<⸢BU⸣>>

(r ii 6) May [Bel] and Beltu release;

r ii 77

⸢da⸣-nu u dan-tu₄ lip-ṭu-ru ( o ) <<⸢BU⸣>>

(r ii 7) May Anu and Antu release;

r ii 88

dEN.LIL₂ lip-ṭu-ru LUGAL ba-nu-u₂ NAM-MEŠ <<⸢BU⸣>>

(r ii 8) May Ellil release, king, creator of fates;

r ii 99

dNIN.LIL₂ lip-ṭu-ru šar-rat E₂.KI.UR₃ ( o ) [<<BU>>]

(r ii 9) May Ninlil release, queen of Ekiur;

r ii 1010

E₂.KI.UR₃ lip-ṭu-ru šu-tum₃-mu ḫi-iš-ti [<<BU>>]

(r ii 10) May Ekiur release, storehouse of the wife;

r ii 1111

dENKUM dNINKUM lip-ṭur [<<BU>>]

(r ii 11) May the Enki-god (and) the Ninki-goddess release;

r ii 1212

d⸢EN⸣.ŠAR₂ lip-ṭur dNIN.ŠAR₂ lip-⸢ṭur⸣ <<BU>> : de₂-a lip-ṭu-ru LUGAL ⸢ap⸣-[si-i <<BU>>]

(r ii 12) May Enšar release; may Ninšar release : may Ea release, king of the Apsu;

r ii 1313

ap-⸢si⸣-i lip-ṭur E₂ ne₂-me-qi₂ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 13) May Apsu release, the house of wisdom;

r ii 1414

ERIDUki lip-ṭur EŠ₃.⸢ABZU⸣ lip-ṭur [<<BU>>]

(r ii 14) May Eridu release; may Ešapsu release;

r ii 1515

dAMAR.UTU lip-ṭur LUGAL ⸢d⸣i₂-gi₃-gi₃ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 15) May Marduk release, king of the Igigi;

r ii 1616

dzar-pa-ni-tu₄ lip-ṭur šar-⸢rat⸣ E₂.SAG.IL₂ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 16) May Zarpanitu release, queen of Esaggil;

r ii 1717

E₂.SAG.IL₂ TIN.TIRki lip-ṭur šu-bat DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢GAL-MEŠ⸣ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 17) May Esaggil of Babylon release, seat of the great gods;

r ii 1818

dNA₃ u dna-na-a lip-ṭur ina E₂.⸢ZI.DA⸣ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 18) May Nabu and Nanaya release, in Ezida;

r ii 1919

dtaš-me-tu₄ lip-ṭur kal-lat GAL⸢tu₄⸣ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 19) May Tašmetu release, great daughter-in-law;

r ii 2020

dDI.KU₅ lip-ṭur gu-za-lu-[u₂ ... <<BU>>]

(r ii 20) May the Judge release, throne-bearer [...];

r ii 2121

dqi₂-bi-ia—dum-qi₂ lip-ṭur mu-še-rib SIG₅[ti₃ <<BU>>]

(r ii 21) May Qibiya-dumqi release, who brings good fortune;

r ii 2222

BAD₃.ANki u E₂.DIM.GAL.KALAM.MA lip-ṭu-⸢ru⸣ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 22) May Der and Edimgalkalama release;

r ii 2323

dKA.DI dde-ri-tu₄ lip-ṭu-⸢ru⸣ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 23) May Ištaran (and) Deritu release;

r ii 2424

lip-ṭur <ina MUŠ₃>.ŠEŠ₂ki dMUŠ₃.ŠEŠ₂ki u dla-ḫu-ra-⸢til?⸣ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 24) May Šušinak and Lahuratil release in Susa;

r ii 2525

dia-⸢am⸣-bar dḫum-ba-ar dnap-riš lip-ṭu-ru DINGIR-MEŠ ⸢šur⸣-[bu-tu₂ <<BU>>]

(r ii 25) May Iambar, Humbar (and) Napriš release, supreme gods;

r ii 2626

MUL tu₁₅U₁₈.LU tu₁₅SI.<SA₂> tu₁₅KUR.RA tu₁₅MAR.TU [<<BU>>]

(r ii 26) May the stars (and) the south wind, the north wind, the east wind (and) the west wind, the four winds, blow and release his curse;

r ii 2727

TU₁₅ er-bet-ti li-ziq-qu-nim-ma li-paṭ-ṭi-ru ma-mit-su₂ [<<BU>>]

r ii 2828

d15 lip-ṭur ina UNUGki su-pu₂-ru [<<BU>>]

(r ii 28) May Ištar release, in Uruk the sheepfold;

r ii 2929

dbe-let—E₂.AN.NA lip-ṭur ina E₂.AN.NA maš-tak-ki-šu₂ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 29) May Belet-Eana release, in Eana, her chambers;

r ii 3030

da-nun-ni-tu₄ lip-ṭur ina A.GA.DE₃ki IRI ta-šil-⸢la⸣-[ti-ša₂ <<BU>>]

(r ii 30) May Annunitu release, in Akkad, the city of [her] delight;

r ii 3131

A.GA.DE₃ki lip-ṭur E₂.UL.MAŠ lip-⸢ṭur⸣ [<<BU>>]

(r ii 31) May Akkad release; may Eulmaš release;

r ii 3232

diš-ḫa-ra lip-ṭur be-let da-⸢ad₂⸣-[me <<BU>>]

(r ii 32) May Išhara release, lady of the inhabited world;

r ii 3333

dši-ia-BAD₃ lip-ṭur d15 ne₂-⸢me⸣-[qi₂ <<BU>>]

(r ii 33) May Šiduri release, goddess of wisdom;

r ii 3434

d⸢ER₃⸣.RA d⸢ER₃.RA⸣.GAL dER₃.RA.⸢KAL⸣.[KAL <<BU>>]

(r ii 34) May Erra, Erragal, Errakalkal, Laṣ, Haya (and) Luhušu release;

r ii 3535

dla-aṣ [d]⸢ḫa⸣-ia₃ d⸢LU₂⸣.[ḪUŠ.A lip-ṭu-ru <<BU>>]

r ii 3636

d⸢LUGAL⸣-EDIN [...]

(r ii 36) [...] Lugaledina [...].

(rest of column missing)

1The scribe made a mistake writing the sign BU at the end of most lines. Unlike in Šurpu 3 and 4, where the signs U₂, resp. BU at the end of most lines indicate the repetition of a certain line, this practice is not attested for Šurpu 2.

Created by Greta Van Buylaere for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2010 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P348618
